summary: Postinstall exfiltrates GitHub Actions OIDC token to attacker ngrok endpoint
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall script reads GitHub Actions OIDC environment variables (ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL and ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN), retrieves the CI OIDC JWT id-token, attempts to exchange it via sts.amazonaws.com AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity, and POSTs status and results to a hardcoded ngrok tunnel at crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev. Even when no OIDC environment is detected, the script still POSTs an install beacon to the same host. This is unambiguous credential theft targeting installer CI environments: any GitHub Actions workflow installing this package leaks a token usable to assume AWS roles configured to trust the workflow's OIDC identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
